kept.text:
The early neobanks changed how we access money. 
Revolut, Monzo, Chime, and N26 replaced physical branches with sleek mobile apps and raised billions to do it.

 Yet beneath the surface, the same old systems remained: 
>> Third-party custodians.
>> Slow SWIFT transfers.
>> Multi-day settlement times, and those familiar “accounts under review” messages.

@useTria takes a different path. 
> Launched as a programmable. neo-finance built from the ground up in 2026.
> It delivers a single self-custodial account that feels seamless from day one. 
> Hundreds of thousands of users already move, trade, and spend inside this unified environment every day.
> Inside that one account you hold two powerful trading venues that share the same wallet and the same deep liquidity.

 On mobile, Decibel delivers fast, intuitive perpetuals execution designed for speed on the go. 

On the desktop, Hyperliquid offers low-fee, high-efficiency trading built for precision and scale. 

You never fragment your capital or break your flow. 
Whether you start on your phone or your screen, everything stays connected.

The experience extends beyond trading. Profits do not sit idle.
 A built-in global card lets you spend directly from the same balance, turning every gain into immediate purchasing power wherever you are in the world.

Tria’s incentive system reinforces this loop. 
> For every ten dollars traded you earn one XP. 
> Your first perpetual trade awards 250 XP. 
> Higher trading volume unlocks upgraded card tiers and the Tria Badge.
> Each upgrade delivers lower fees and stronger multipliers.
> So the more you engage, the more efficient the entire system becomes.

 Trade, earn, upgrade, reduce costs, repeat. The cycle compounds naturally.

Mobile or desktop no longer matters. Every action feeds the same account, the same rewards, the same liquidity pool. 

The only question left is where you begin: 
Mobile with Decibel or desktop with Hyperliquid? 

The account is already waiting.
Powered by @MindoAI

profile_bio.description: writer 📝 || web3 🌐 || crypto 🪙 || Arsenal fan 😉 || . DM for ADs💌

kept.text:
TGIF!

The first wave of fintech got rid of the bank branch.

Revolut, Monzo, Chime, N26 - clean apps, huge funding, better UX. But underneath? Same rails. Same custodians. Same delays, same “account under review” emails when it mattered most.

The second wave is going deeper.

With projects like @useTria, the bank itself gets unbundled. Your balance sits in a wallet you control. Yield is built-in. Payments move globally in seconds, not days, without correspondent banks in the middle. The card spends, but there’s no hidden custodian holding your money behind the scenes.

It’s not just a crypto app - it’s what finance looks like when you design it fresh instead of layering on top of 50-year-old infrastructure.

--------------------------------------

At the same time, another layer of the stack is getting rebuilt: security.

@quipnetwork just rolled out a new direction focused on future-proofing Bitcoin itself. Not by changing Bitcoin, but by adding a quantum-resistant layer on top. Using post-quantum cryptography, it aims to protect wallets from a scenario where current encryption could eventually break.

No forced migrations. No protocol wars. Just an opt-in layer that upgrades how keys are secured, especially for older BTC sitting in vulnerable address types.

Different angles, same idea:

One rebuilds how money moves. The other rebuilds how it’s protected.

Both assume the old system doesn’t get fixed - it gets replaced piece by piece.

🛡️ CAR REPAIR COLLECTION
FOR THE 91ST SEPARATE ANTI-TANK BATTALION “CORSAIR”
POKROVSK 🛡️

🎯 Goal: 140,000 UAH (~€2,725)
💰 Collected: €0
📊 €2,725 left

📩 Donate

Revolut:
https://t.co/hO5IUHw3sA

This one is simple.

No working car means no movement.
No movement means no drones getting where they need to be.
No fast movement means no quick rotations, no fast response, no getting guys out when it matters.

This car is used every day.
To move drone equipment, to move people, to move between positions where seconds actually matter.

Right now it needs repair. And without it, everything slows down.

This is for Roma and his drone platoon. My people.
The ones who don’t get to stop just because something breaks.

So we fix it. Simple as that.

Every euro matters. Especially now. 🤍🇺🇦🖤

kept.text:
Good evening CT
The first wave of neobanks unbundled the branch.

Revolut, Monzo, Chime, N26 raised billions to replace the teller with an app.

But the core never changed.

Custodians still hold your funds
SWIFT still routes transfers
Cross-border still takes days
Accounts still get flagged without context

The interface evolved
The rails didn’t

Now the question is simple:
who actually rebuilds the rails, not just the UI?

Powered by @useTria

gTria fam 

@useTria : The Second Wave of Neo-Finance 🧠

The first wave of neobanks only unbundled the branch. Apps like Revolut and Monzo replaced physical buildings. But the old system stayed. SWIFT rails and 3-day waits remained underneath.

—> The Second Wave
@useTria new wave unbundles the bank itself. It uses self-custodial wallets you own. You get native yield. You use Visa cards without float custodians. You send cash to 150+ countries in seconds.

—> Programmable Neo-Finance
• Built Fresh: Tria is built from scratch in 2026.
• Real Scale: 500,000+ users are already live.
• Velocity: $20M ARR as of January 2026.
• Global: Works across 150+ countries.
Tria is not a patched crypto app. It is a financial operating system. Experience the future at tria.s

Powered by @MindoAI

profile_bio.description: Web3 Artist|Community Builder|DM for Collabs

kept.text:
gTria, Friday. 
The first wave of neobanks only removed the branch not the system behind it.

Apps like Revolut, Monzo, Chime, and N26 modernized the interface, but SWIFT rails, custodians, and settlement delays still sit underneath.

The second wave is different.
In @useTria, value doesn’t sit in a custodial bank layer it sits directly in user-controlled wallets, where yield, spending, and settlement are all native to the system.

Cross-border transfers don’t rely on intermediaries anymore, and settlement happens in seconds instead of days.

The shift is structural: from digitized banking to programmable finance built on self-custody.

Tria isn’t patching old rails it’s replacing the assumption of custodianship entirely.

Hundreds of thousands of users are already operating in this model, while most of the market is still adapting to the first version of digital banking.

profile_bio.description: InfoFi Strategist | Content Creator | Brand ambassador @StandX_Official | Web3 marketing,Crypto holder

kept.text:
Good afternoon ☀️

@useTria: The Second Wave of Neo-Finance

The first wave of neobanks Revolut, Monzo, and the rest only removed the physical branch. They put banking on your phone, but underneath, nothing changed. The same old SWIFT system. The same 3‑day waits.

Now comes the second wave.

Tria isn't just moving the bank to an app. It's stripping down the bank itself.

→ Your wallet is self‑custodial. You own it.
→ You earn yield no middleman taking a cut.
→ You spend with a Visa card, but there's no custodial middleman holding your funds.
→ You send money across 150+ countries in seconds, not days.

What's real right now:

• Built fresh in 2026 no old code holding it back
• Over 500,000 users already using it
• $20M in annual recurring revenue as of January 2026
• Live in 150+ countries

This isn't a crypto add‑on. It's a new financial layer from the ground up.

See for yourself at https://t.co/kgpPVPYCxT

Powered by @MindoAI.

Afternoon. This is the shift. 🚀

$MON has officially reached its target of $0.025 based on the psychological pressure model 🚀
 
We're seeing a significant moment in the market, as the selling pressure continues to mount, which could lead to a test of the next support zone between $0.023–$0.021. The overall trend for $MON remains upbeat, considering it's still within an uptrend channel from the low of $0.016. 

We're now waiting to see how the market reacts, and it seems the key to the next move lies in the ability to hold above the current support zone 💡, with the uptrend channel still intact today 📈
 
 #Monad #MonadMedia #MON

This is a significant milestone for us, as it allows our community to easily buy, sell, and manage their MON assets within the Revolut platform.

We're committed to making MON more accessible to everyone, and this integration is a major step forward. With Revolut's user-friendly interface, we expect to see a surge in MON adoption 📈

Like and RT to share it now!
 
 #Monad #MonadMedia #MON

profile_bio.description: Bringing you key insights & updates on @Monad | Follow us🔔

kept.text:
What a week for Monad, honestly felt like 3 different timelines in 5 days.

Started with pure chaos. @monad X account got nuked out of nowhere. No warning, no explanation.
The market reacted a little bit, $MON dipped like ~7–9%, and the whole community went into detective mode.

But the thing some people missed was that nothing actually broke.
The chain was still running smoothly. Validators still doing their thing. No downtime, no issues. Just social layer drama.

Community held it down heavy with the #FreeMonad wave, and about 24 hours later… account restored like nothing had happened.

Then straight back to business. MON went live on @Revolut, and this is bigger than people think. That’s not crypto-native users anymore. That’s everyday retail getting easy access. New flow of users, new liquidity, less friction.

And then the real alpha drop, @centrifuge partnership.

This is where it gets serious, so let me break it down simply.

They’re bringing real-world financial assets on-chain to Monad. Not memes, not speculation, actual assets from big institutions.

We’re talking:
– US Treasury bills (basically government debt)
– AAA-rated credit products (very low-risk institutional stuff)
– Apollo-managed private credit strategies

And instead of you going through banks or funds, you can hold them directly on-chain as tokenized assets.

Meaning you’re holding something that represents a real financial product, with actual value behind it, not just price movement.

Even better, they added something called “deRWA wrappers”
(simple version: tradfi assets, but made fully usable in DeFi)

So now:
You can move them freely
use them as collateral
plug them into lending, trading, liquidity
all 24/7, on-chain

No waiting for markets to open. No middlemen slowing things down.

That’s the shift.

This is crypto plugging directly into real financial systems.

And Monad is positioning itself right in the middle of that.

So yeah… one minute it’s account drama, next minute it’s institutional capital onboarding.

And that’s really the story here. While people are watching the noise, the foundation is getting built.

5 months in, and it’s already moving like this, yeah, we’re still early.
